data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

File-disjointness: 8937 file_impact = _snapshot_schema.py (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py (new), docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md (new), fetcher.py (producer validates vs schema). DISJOINT from every sibling — fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (tests/_jira_shape_contract.py, jira_contract/test_verified_fake_contract.py, acli_graph.py read-only), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (test_roundtrip.py/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh/docs/jira-sync-setup.md). No sibling co-edits fetcher.py or the new files; fetcher.py is only READ by fe57's fake via fetch_snapshot. 8937 declares depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 8937's files are net-new or solely-owned (fetcher.py). The FakeAcliClient/fixtures are PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 8937 through fetch_snapshot over the declared depends_on fe57 edge — clean producer/consumer split; no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins the inbound read-side shapes (comment nested / issuelinks / parent / scalars); fe57 AC2 (fake serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation) feeds exactly those — mutually reinforcing with 59ef (return-shape fidelity) and 822a (behavioral round-trip). The epic's one real representation conflict (outbound labels list-vs-int, localized to 33d0↔2359 and resolved as-shipped via integer counters) is outbound-telemetry-internal; 8937's read-side schema neither asserts nor depends on labels_applied, so it is untouched.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937's only input dependency (fe57's fixtures/fake) is declared. Fixtures (→fe57), applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359) are explicitly scoped OUT, consistent with the roster. The round-trip "…→ applier" binds to "the production path" abstractly (the batch path is already production and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction), so no hard 33d0-before-8937 edge is required. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip; 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity; 33d0 = outbound-only APPLICATION correctness; 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL behavioral ROUND-TRIP. Distinct files, complementary angles. The anti-change-detector guardrail is assigned to 8937 BY NAME ("owned by story B") + AC5, an in-scope deliverable, not a sibling overlap.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937 does not edit the applier files, so it is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / lean-suite intent. No sibling forced into a conflicting conformance.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface (fetch_snapshot writes + inbound/outbound differ reads). 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier, but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION path, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

NOTE: the documented anti-change-detector guardrail is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937) per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause + 8937 AC5 (shipped as docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md) — an in-scope deliverable, neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency.

VERDICT (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
